channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Federal Government Boosts Nigeria’s Tourism and Hospitality Standards
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don really dey push to improve the tourism and hospitality industry to make am dey competitive globally. During one big event at Merit House, Abuja, the National Inst...

item.title: Most Migrants Verified as Legally in South Africa After Durban Operation
item.description: In Durban, authorities conducted a verification operation at the Diakonia Centre following reports that over 500 migrants were displaced from their homes by anti-immigration groups. eThekwini Mayor Cy...

item.title: Meta reaches settlement with Kentucky school district over social media addiction claims
item.description: Meta, along with other major social media companies including TikTok, Snap, and YouTube, has reached a settlement with the Breathitt County School District in Kentucky over claims related to social me...

item.title: Fritz Lang’s Vision of 2026 in Metropolis: A Century-Old Sci-Fi Prediction of Urban Life
item.description: In Fritz Lang’s 1927 cinematic masterpiece Metropolis, the year 2026 is depicted as a highly structured, futuristic city where architecture mirrors social hierarchy. Lang envisioned a towering metropo...

item.title: Sundowns Aim for CAF Champions League Triumph with Strong Team Spirit

item.title: Trump supporters express concern over DJT stock amid market gains
item.description: Donald Trump's recent post on Truth Social celebrating a strong stock market was met with frustration by some of his supporters, who are concerned about the declining value of Trump Media & Technology...

item.title: Rising Gas Prices Contribute to Increased Retail Sales in March
item.description: In March 2026, Canadian retail sales experienced a notable increase, largely driven by higher spending at gas stations. As fuel prices rose across the country, consumers spent more at service stations...

item.title: England Manager Tuchel Makes Surprising Squad Changes, Omitting Several Star Players
item.description: England football fans are shocked after Thomas Tuchel, the new national team manager, left out some big names from the squad. Chelsea’s Cole Palmer, Manchester City’s Phil Foden, Liverpool’s Trent Ale...

item.title: How the Publishing Industry is Adapting to AI in Writing
item.description: The article discusses the concept of the 'literary uncanny valley,' a term describing the uneasy feeling readers experience when engaging with AI-generated writing. Just as robots or animations that a...

item.title: Ex-aide warns of Trump's potential international focus if GOP loses 2026 midterms
item.description: Former Homeland Security official Miles Taylor warns that if the Republican Party faces losses in the 2026 midterm elections, President Donald Trump could redirect his focus internationally. Drawing o...

item.title: 9/11 Memorial & Museum Provides Free Admission to Veterans to Honor Post-9/11 Service
item.description: Ahead of Memorial Day, the 9/11 Memorial & Museum in New York announced it will provide free admission to all veterans, expanding its existing program for active-duty military personnel. This initiati...

item.title: Tippi Hedren’s Elegant Pre-Hollywood Photography by Genevieve Naylor
item.description: Before Tippi Hedren became widely recognized as the iconic Hitchcock blonde, she enjoyed a thriving modeling career in New York City during the 1950s. In 1954, she collaborated with Genevieve Naylor, ...

item.title: Association Between Proximity to Nuclear Power Plants and Cancer Mortality in the U.S., 2000–2020
item.description: A recent national study analyzed the relationship between living near nuclear power plants and mortality from lung, breast, and colon cancers in the United States over a twenty-year period. Researcher...

item.title: Adolf Galland’s Unconventional Lobster Delivery Flight During WWII
item.description: In April 1941, German Luftwaffe ace Adolf Galland, then a Lieutenant Colonel commanding the JG 26 fighter wing, orchestrated an unusual mission: personally delivering a gourmet seafood basket to celeb...

item.title: Kenya's proposed excise duty may hurt Tanzania’s glass exports and regional trade
item.description: Tanzania’s leading glass company, KIOO Limited, don express serious concern over Kenya’s plan to impose a 35 percent excise duty on glass bottles imported from East African Community (EAC) countries. ...

item.title: Mudavadi Urges Kenyans to Prepare for Economic and Social Challenges
item.description: Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi has called on Kenyans to mentally and practically prepare for upcoming economic and social challenges. Speaking in Nairobi, Mudavadi noted that the government ...

item.description: The Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines (CBCP) has called on all parishes nationwide to recite a special prayer titled “A Nation’s Prayer for Enlightenment, Conversion and Renewal” in resp...

item.title: US Publishes Second Set of Declassified UFO Reports
item.description: The United States Defence Department has unveiled a second batch of previously classified documents relating to UFO sightings, known officially as 'unidentified anomalous phenomena'. The disclosure in...

item.title: Saudi Arabia Introduces Smart Watches to Track Heart Health of Pilgrims During Hajj
item.description: During the 2026 Hajj, Saudi Arabia don start to use smart watch technology to dey monitor pilgrims wey get heart conditions. The Madinah Health Cluster join forces with Seha Virtual Hospital put this ...
